What I'd say to R.

I've been thinking about your question, and my honest answer is "yes, but deliberately, not just because it's the next title." A few things I've noticed: the judgment moments — watching you handle that tense sponsor call, the Vantage escalation — are the parts I find most interesting, not the parts I dread. That makes me think the PM direction genuinely fits me, not just my CV.

I also want to be clear-eyed that PM isn't just a bigger coordinator job. You own the outcome and answer to the sponsor; I support and produce under your direction. The bit I'm least sure about is carrying the accountability publicly — being the one a wrong call lands on. I won't know how I handle that until I've felt some of it.

So what I'd ask: could I take on a couple of stretch pieces over the next few months? Drafting the next sponsor pre-read for you to review, and maybe leading one of the smaller workshops end-to-end. That would let me test the parts I haven't done — and test whether I actually like the accountability, not just the idea of it.

I think that's a sensible next step rather than asking for a title I'm not yet ready for.
